entries.text: Finished rereading 'Letters from a Stoic' on the run. Relevant reflections on wasted time resonate with current challenges in the Cato chapter.
Finished rereading 'Letters from a Stoic' by Seneca during the run. The section on wasted time resonated strongly, especially while working through challenges with the Cato chapter.
Finished rereading Letters from a Stoic on the run today. Good timing given the work on the Cato chapter. Seneca's reflections on wasted time resonated deeply this read.
Finished rereading 'Letters from a Stoic' on today's run. It came at a good time, given the challenges with the Cato chapter. Seneca's reflections on wasted time resonated deeply during this read.

Had a rough night with only about five hours of sleep. Despite that, managed to get the walk in with the boys. Liam found a turtle near the mailbox, which was a sweet moment. Writing was tough today, especially trying to get movement on the Cato section. Felt like pushing through mud.

Finished rereading 'Letters from a Stoic' on the run today. The timing felt particularly apt given the current work on the Cato chapter. Seneca's thoughts on wasted time resonated differently during this reading.

Rough night with only about five hours of sleep. Managed to get the morning walk with the boys; Liam found a turtle near the mailbox which was a nice moment. Writing session felt difficult, like pushing through mud, particularly struggling with the Cato section.

Finished rereading 'Letters from a Stoic' while on a run today. The insights felt particularly relevant given the struggles with the Cato chapter. Seneca's thoughts on wasting time stood out more this time.
Rough night, five hours of sleep. Morning walk with Liam and Theo, Liam found a turtle near the mailbox. Writing felt like pushing through mud, couldn't get the Cato section to move.

Rough night, maybe five hours of sleep. Felt tired. Got the morning walk in with Liam and Theo, and Liam found a turtle near the mailbox. Writing felt like pushing through mud, specifically the Cato section wasn't moving.

Finished rereading Seneca's "Letters from a Stoic" during an afternoon run. The material on wasted time felt particularly impactful, especially given the current difficulties with the Cato chapter in my writing.
Rough night — maybe five hours. Got the walk in with Liam and Theo; Liam found a turtle near the mailbox. Writing this morning felt like pushing through mud. Couldn't get the Cato section to move.

2026-05-19 morning notes: Slept roughly five hours (fitful). Still managed 6:00 a.m. walk with Liam and Theo; highlight: Liam discovered a turtle near the mailbox — boys fascinated, paused to watch it retract and re-emerge. Writing block afterwards felt heavy, particularly the Cato subsection; metaphorical mud. No clear structural breakthrough.

Finished a reread of Seneca's Letters from a Stoic on today's run in Bastrop State Park. Noted that Seneca on wasted time hit differently this read — connected it to what he's working through with the Cato chapter of Discipline Is Destiny.

Finished rereading Letters from a Stoic during today's run. Seneca's passage on wasted time struck me harder than previous reads, especially given the ongoing struggle with the Cato chapter structure. Noted a sense that I'm letting the draft ramble—too many anecdotes, not enough argument. The line about "life is long if you know how to use it" felt like a direct admonishment.
Rough night — about five hours. Got the walk in with the boys; Liam found a turtle near the mailbox. Morning writing felt like pushing through mud. The Cato section still wouldn't move.

Rough night — maybe five hours of sleep. Still got the sunrise walk in with Liam and Theo; highlight was Liam spotting a small box turtle right by the mailbox. Logged it carefully and let the boys watch it crawl off into the grass.

Sat down for the Cato section after journaling but the pages felt like moving through mud. Nothing flowed. The argument still isn't landing. Low energy, low focus. Seneca's line about "when you are tired, reason is tired too" kept floating up.

Need to sort out sleep tonight or tomorrow will be the same grind.

Finished a reread of Seneca's Letters from a Stoic during today's run. The passages on wasted time landed harder than they have in the past—feels like a mirror held up to the stalled Cato section. Noticing how fatigue blurs the edges of discipline.

Rough night of sleep, about 5 hours. Got the morning walk in with the boys. Liam found a turtle near the mailbox. Writing this morning felt like pushing through mud; couldn't get the Cato section to move.
Finished rereading Letters from a Stoic on the run today. Good timing with the Cato chapter work. Seneca's reflections on wasted time hit differently this time around.

Rough night of sleep, maybe five hours. Still got the morning walk in with the boys, and Liam found a turtle near the mailbox. Writing this morning felt like pushing through mud—couldn't get the Cato section to move.
Finished rereading Letters from a Stoic on the run today. Good timing given the current struggles with the Cato chapter. Seneca on wasted time hit differently this read.

Rough night — maybe five hours. Did the walk with the boys anyway. Liam found a turtle near the mailbox, which is the kind of thing that makes the walk worth doing regardless of everything else. Writing this morning was slow and resistant. The Cato section isn't yielding. Second day in a row of this particular combination: short sleep, stuck chapter, mud-feeling. The pattern is consistent — under six hours and the cards don't want to move.

Rough night — maybe five hours. Morning walk still happened: Liam and Theo in the stroller, down to the mailbox and back. Liam found a turtle near the mailbox. One of those moments that cuts through everything else. Writing afterward felt like pushing through mud. The Cato section is stuck again — third day now. The argument isn't forming on the cards the way it needs to.
